cadaverous.def: 像屍體的、蒼白的、形同枯槁的
cadaverous.ex: The prisoner had a thin, cadaverous face.

caddie/caddy.def: (高爾夫)球僮、茶葉罐、當球僮的人的人物、球僮地 (球僮)
caddie/caddy.ex: The golf caddie carried the player's clubs all day.

caddish.def: 卑鄙的、無禮的、無賴地 (卑鄙)
caddish.ex: His caddish behavior alienated most of his friends.

cadence.def: 抑揚頓挫、節奏、降調、調的人、節律地 (節奏)
cadence.ex: She spoke with the rhythmic cadence of a professional actor.

cadet.def: 學員、幼子、次子的人物、學員地 (學員)
cadet.col: military cadet, police cadet, naval cadet
cadet.ex: The military cadet marched with great pride.

cadge.def: 乞討、強求、乞討的人的人、乞討地 (乞)
cadge.ex: He's always trying to cadge a free meal from us.

cadmium.ex: Cadmium is a highly toxic heavy metal.

caesarian/caesarean.ex: She had to have a caesarian section for her first child.

café/cafe.ex: Let's meet at the sidewalk cafe for breakfast.

cafeteria.ex: The university cafeteria serves lunch from 11 to 2.

caffeine.ex: Tea and coffee both contain a reasonable amount of caffeine.

caftan/kaftan.def: 卡夫坦長袍、長袍的人 (或物)、長袍地 (袍)
caftan/kaftan.ex: She wore a colorful silk caftan during the summer gala.

cage.def: 籠子、牢房、關進籠子、囚禁的人的人物、囚禁地 (籠)
cage.col: bird cage, tiger cage, golden cage
cage.ex: The lion paced back and forth in its small cage.

caged.def: 被關在籠中的、拘束的人的人 (或物)
caged.ex: I don't like to see caged animals in the zoo.

cagey/cagy.def: (口)謹慎的、機靈的、狡猾地 (謹慎)
cagey/cagy.ex: He is very cagey about his past as a merchant.

cajole.def: 勾引、誘騙、哄騙的人的人、誘騙地 (騙)
cajole.ex: He tried to cajole me into lending him money.

cajoling.ex: She used her cajoling manner to get what she wanted.

cake.def: 蛋糕、塊狀物、結塊、塗弄、蛋糕的人的人、結塊地 (蛋糕)
cake.col: birthday cake, piece of cake, take the cake (絕了/最糟的一位)
cake.ex: She baked a delicious chocolate cake for the party.

caked.def: 結塊的、覆蓋著...的的人 (或物) (塊)
caked.ex: His boots were caked with dried red mud.

calabash.ex: In some cultures, a dried calabash is used to carry water.

calamine.def: 異極礦、(藥)爐甘石的人 (或物) (藥)
calamine.ex: Calamine lotion is good for relieving the itch of insect bites.

calamitous.ex: The expedition ended in a calamitous failure.

calamity.ex: The sudden flood was a major calamity for the farmers.

calcareous.ex: The region is known for its white calcareous cliffs.

calcification.ex: Calcification of the arteries is a common sign of aging.

calcified.def: 鈣化的、僵化的的人 (或物) (鈣化)
calcified.ex: The ancient tree trunk had become almost calcified over time.

calcify.def: 鈣化、使硬化、鈣化的人的人、鈣化地 (化)
calcify.ex: The soft tissue gradually began to calcify after the injury.

calcium.ex: Milk is a very good source of natural calcium.

calculable.def: 可計算的、可預見的、可靠的人 (或物) (算)
calculable.ex: The company decided to take a small, calculable risk.
